name: Alliance Community Bank
description_short: Alliance Community Bank is a regional bank with 6 branches across central Illinois offering checking, savings, CDs, mortgages, consumer loans, and agricultural financing.
description_long:
Alliance Community Bank operates as a traditional community bank serving the central Illinois region, with a primary presence in Petersburg, Springfield, Athens, and Mason City. The bank has established itself as a local financial institution with multiple branch locations and a physical presence spanning Menard and Sangamon counties. Founded to serve the banking needs of its regional community, the bank maintains traditional branch operations alongside digital banking capabilities.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail and commercial banking services. Deposit products include checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit (CDs), with featured 24-month CDs advertised at 4.07% APY. On the lending side, Alliance Community Bank provides mortgage lending for home purchases and refinancing, consumer loans for personal credit needs, and specialized agricultural loans tailored to the farming community. The institution also provides mobile banking through iOS and Android apps, online banking access, and debit card services.

Alliance Community Bank distinguishes itself through its localized community focus, maintaining six physical branch locations with extended drive-up hours (some until 5:30 PM weekdays). The bank actively engages in community events, as evidenced by social media posts about local fundraising initiatives. The institution holds NMLS # 462014 and operates under routing number 071111481, indicating full regulatory registration. Their willingness to invest in system upgrades (as announced in their digital banking maintenance notice) suggests ongoing modernization efforts.

As a regional community bank, Alliance Community Bank serves customers who value personal relationships and local decision-making, though their product offerings and rates are comparable to other mid-sized regional banks. The bank operates standard banking hours with lobby service Monday-Friday 8-4 PM and Saturday mornings, limiting accessibility for customers with non-traditional schedules. Like most community banks, they likely have higher minimum balances and fewer premium products compared to national institutions, though specific terms are not detailed on their primary website pages provided. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Six conveniently located branch offices across central Illinois (Petersburg, Springfield, Athens, Mason City, New Salem, Yucan Drive)
Extended drive-up hours until 5:30 PM on weekdays at select locations for working customers
Competitive CD rates featured at 4.07% APY for 24-month terms
Full suite of lending products including mortgage, consumer, and agricultural loans
Mobile banking apps available on both iOS and Android platforms
Saturday lobby hours available at most locations (8 AM - Noon)
Direct phone access and established routing number for easy transfers
cons: Limited lobby hours (closes at 4 PM weekdays) for customers unable to visit during business hours
Digital banking was temporarily unavailable during April system upgrade with multi-day restoration window
Agricultural loan focus suggests primary market may not include all consumer segments equally
No mention of premium products, business accounts, or specialized services beyond standard offerings
Limited online visibility into specific account terms, minimum balances, or fee structures
best_for: Central Illinois residents seeking personalized banking with local branch relationships
Agricultural business owners and farmers needing specialized farm lending products
Customers who prefer in-person banking and value Saturday lobby hours
Mortgage borrowers in the Springfield, Petersburg, or Athens areas
diagnosis: Alliance Community Bank is best for central Illinois residents who value local banking relationships, convenient branch access, and personalized service from a regional institution. The main caveat is that as a community bank, they have more limited hours and product depth than national banks, making them less suitable for customers needing 24/7 digital-only banking or specialized premium financial products.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
